assessment.text: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped pediatric rash history, distribution, morphology, symptom, exposure, medication, impact, and observed skin fields.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for pediatric-rash telehealth documentation in Dubai; medication and dose passages were reviewed but deliberately not mapped.

assessment.text: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped pediatric fever, pain, immune compromise, uncertainty, worsening, return, and follow-up prompts.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis
source.population: Children and adults with atopic dermatitis complaints.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth atopic-dermatitis documentation; partial for every eczema or contact-dermatitis subtype.

assessment.text: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped fever, painful or rapidly worsening eczema, blister, and infection-warning fields.

assessment.text: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-documented skin care, trigger, medicine, education, and question fields.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis
source.population: Children and adults with atopic dermatitis complaints.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth atopic-dermatitis documentation; partial for every eczema or contact-dermatitis subtype.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

assessment.text: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped breathing and facial or lip swelling warnings only; skin findings alone are explicitly not treated as anaphylaxis.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers
source.population: Adults and children with suspected anaphylaxis.
source.setting: Hospital and out-of-hospital healthcare settings.
source.exclusions: Used only to support anaphylaxis warning documentation associated with rash or urticaria; it does not convert isolated skin findings into anaphylaxis.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.
